# PRD: Phase 2c — Gmail Tools

**Generated by:** Lopmon (CLM Studios)
**Date:** 2026-04-19
**Status:** Approved by Citlali — ready for coding agent
**Requested by:** Citlali (Phase 2 continuation, right after dashboard unblock)

---

## 1. Goal

Make the Executive Assistant and Development Director actually do Gmail work. After this PRD, a user in chat with the EA can say "What's in my inbox?" or "Draft a thank-you reply to that gala donor" and get real results. The Development Director gets the same Gmail tools plus its existing Grants + CRM tools, so donor outreach can flow end-to-end ("find this donor in the CRM → draft an email to them").

This is the third tool family on top of the Phase 2b tool-use loop.

## 2. Why

- OAuth scope `gmail.modify` is already requested at `Connect Google` and access tokens are already stored (AES-256-GCM encrypted) in the `integrations` table. The *capability* exists but no code calls it.
- Vee competitive intel: Vee's only real shipped moat is autonomous social posting. Edify's equivalent moat will be integrated email drafting + donor CRM + grants search as one loop. Gmail is the centerpiece of that.
- Phase 2b established the tool-use loop pattern + registry + addendum system. Phase 2c is additive — we just register new tools.
- Phase 3 already landed Grants + CRM on Development Director. Adding Gmail makes DD's "find grant → find donor → draft outreach" story complete.

## 3. Architecture Decisions

- **Direct REST calls to Gmail v1, no `googleapis` SDK.** Same pattern as `lib/google-calendar.ts`. Endpoints: `https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/...`. `Bearer ${accessToken}` header.
- **Integration type = `"gmail"`** (not `"google_gmail"`). Matches the existing `GOOGLE_INTEGRATION_TYPES` array in `lib/google.ts`. Token retrieval: `getValidGoogleAccessToken(serviceClient, orgId, "gmail")`.
- **Tool names prefix = `gmail_`** so the registry dispatcher's `name.startsWith("gmail_")` branch routes to `executeGmailTool`.
- **Send gating.** `gmail_send_message` always sends immediately; Claude is instructed via the system addendum to *always* show the user the drafted content and get explicit confirmation in-chat before calling `gmail_send_message`. For autonomous safety, `gmail_create_draft` is preferred — it stores as a Gmail draft, user opens Gmail to review/send. Both tools exist; descriptions push Claude toward drafts by default.
- **Slim projections.** `gmail_list_messages` returns only `id`, `threadId`, `snippet`, `from`, `subject`, `date`, `isUnread`. Full body only via `gmail_get_message`. Keeps token usage in check on inbox-heavy accounts.
- **Thread-aware.** `gmail_list_threads` + `gmail_get_thread` exist alongside message tools so multi-turn donor conversations can be summarized without N separate message fetches.
- **Labels.** `gmail_modify_labels` takes `addLabelIds` + `removeLabelIds`. Built-in labels (`INBOX`, `UNREAD`, `STARRED`, `IMPORTANT`, `SPAM`, `TRASH`, `SENT`, `DRAFT`) are accepted directly. Custom labels use their label ID, which the agent can find via `gmail_list_labels`.
- **Max message body size.** `gmail_get_message` truncates plain-text bodies at 8000 chars with a trailing `[...truncated]` marker. Avoids blowing the model's context on a long thread.

## 4. Scope

### In scope

1. **`apps/web/src/lib/google-gmail.ts`** (NEW) — typed REST wrappers around Gmail v1:
   - `listMessages({ accessToken, query?, labelIds?, maxResults? })` → `{ messages: GmailMessage[] }` (default `maxResults: 20`, supports Gmail search syntax via `query` e.g. `"from:donor@example.org is:unread"`)
   - `getMessage({ accessToken, messageId })` → `{ message: GmailMessage }` (full body, headers parsed)
   - `listThreads({ accessToken, query?, labelIds?, maxResults? })` → `{ threads: GmailThread[] }` (default `maxResults: 10`)
   - `getThread({ accessToken, threadId })` → `{ thread: GmailThread }` (all messages in the thread, slim shape)
   - `createDraft({ accessToken, to, subject, body, cc?, bcc?, threadId?, inReplyTo? })` → `{ draft: GmailDraft }` (RFC 2822 MIME, base64url encoded; threadId + inReplyTo headers wire threading correctly)
   - `sendMessage({ accessToken, to, subject, body, cc?, bcc?, threadId?, inReplyTo? })` → `{ message: GmailMessage }`
   - `modifyLabels({ accessToken, messageId, addLabelIds?, removeLabelIds? })` → `{ message: GmailMessage }`
   - `listLabels({ accessToken })` → `{ labels: GmailLabel[] }`
   - Custom `GmailError` class thrown on non-2xx, matching the `GoogleCalendarError` shape
   - Type definitions for `GmailMessage`, `GmailThread`, `GmailDraft`, `GmailLabel` — slim shapes we surface to Claude

2. **`apps/web/src/lib/tools/gmail.ts`** (NEW) — Anthropic tool definitions + executor:
   - 8 `Anthropic.Tool` objects: `gmail_list_messages`, `gmail_get_message`, `gmail_list_threads`, `gmail_get_thread`, `gmail_create_draft`, `gmail_send_message`, `gmail_modify_labels`, `gmail_list_labels`
   - `GMAIL_TOOLS_ADDENDUM` string exported — instructs Claude to prefer `create_draft` over `send_message`, always confirm before sending, and never invent message content
   - `executeGmailTool({ name, input, accessToken })` — switches on name, validates required inputs, catches `GmailError`, returns `{ content, is_error? }`

3. **`apps/web/src/lib/tools/registry.ts`** (MODIFY):
   - Import `gmailTools`, `executeGmailTool`, `GMAIL_TOOLS_ADDENDUM`
   - Re-export `GMAIL_TOOLS_ADDENDUM`
   - Add `"gmail"` to `getToolFamilies` and `buildSystemAddendums`
   - Update `ARCHETYPE_TOOLS`:
     - `executive_assistant: [...calendarTools, ...gmailTools]`
     - `development_director: [...grantsTools, ...crmTools, ...gmailTools]`
     - Other archetypes unchanged for now
   - Add dispatch branch: `if (name.startsWith("gmail_")) { ... getValidGoogleAccessToken(..., "gmail") → executeGmailTool(...) }` — mirrors the calendar branch exactly, including `preFetchedTokens?.get("gmail")` support

4. **`apps/web/src/app/api/team/[slug]/chat/route.ts`** (MODIFY): token pre-fetching
   - If the tool call list for a round contains any `gmail_*` tool, fetch `gmail` token once and pass via `preFetchedTokens.set("gmail", ...)`
   - Mirrors the existing calendar pre-fetch pattern

5. **System prompt addendum** — new `GMAIL_TOOLS_ADDENDUM`:
   ```
   You have access to the user's Gmail via tools. Prefer gmail_create_draft over gmail_send_message unless the user explicitly asks you to send immediately — drafts let the user review before sending. Never fabricate email content; always call gmail_list_messages or gmail_get_message to see what's actually there. When replying, use threadId and inReplyTo so Gmail threads the response correctly.
   ```

### Out of scope (later)

- Attachments (upload + download). Multipart MIME is non-trivial — defer until there's a real use case.
- Push notifications (Gmail watch + Pub/Sub). Polling is fine for MVP.
- Email templates / merge variables — Claude can synthesize from CRM data directly in the draft.
- Search operators beyond Gmail's native `query` syntax — pass-through is enough.
- Drive tools (Phase 2d).
- Any non-EA, non-Development Director archetype getting Gmail tools.

## 5. Acceptance Criteria

Done when:
1. ✅ A user with Google connected can chat with the EA and ask "What's unread in my inbox?" — gets a real, slim message list
2. ✅ User can ask "Draft a thank-you reply to the last email from donor@example.org" — a real Gmail draft is created, user confirms seeing it in Gmail
3. ✅ Without Google connected, EA and DD return the "Google Workspace not connected" friendly error
4. ✅ Development Director has Gmail + Grants + CRM tools simultaneously (verify via registry)
5. ✅ Marketing, Programs, Events, HR archetypes do NOT get Gmail tools
6. ✅ Token pre-fetch in chat route deduplicates Gmail token lookups when multiple `gmail_*` calls happen in the same round
7. ✅ `npm run build` passes with no type errors
8. ✅ /simplify pass run after the feature commit
9. ✅ Single commit on `main`, message: `feat: Gmail tools (Phase 2c)`

## 6. Risks & Open Questions

- **`gmail.modify` is a sensitive scope.** Already in the OAuth consent screen. No new Google verification needed — the test-user allowlist still works. When Z submits for verification, `gmail.modify` is in the scope set.
- **Send-message can be misused by a confused model.** Mitigations: (a) addendum pushes toward drafts, (b) tool description on `gmail_send_message` explicitly says "only after user confirms the full drafted content in chat," (c) no in-code gate — we trust the model + user to coordinate. If this becomes a support issue, add a confirm-token pattern in a follow-up.
- **MIME encoding.** Gmail v1 requires RFC 2822 base64url-encoded messages. The agent should write a small helper in `google-gmail.ts` — don't reach for a library.
- **Label IDs vs names.** Built-in labels use canonical names (`INBOX`, etc.). Custom labels require the label's ID (opaque string). `gmail_list_labels` exposes the mapping so Claude can look it up when a user references a label by name.
- **Existing grep hit on `integrations-page/page.tsx`.** That's an older static-export placeholder. Leave it alone — actual integration UI lives in `dashboard/integrations/page.tsx`.

## 7. Estimated Effort

~30 min agent + ~10 min /simplify pass.

## 8. Approval

- [x] Citlali approved (2026-04-19 via Telegram — "Lets hit the Phase 2c Gmail and Demo bypass gate first")
- [x] Architecture pre-locked (mirrors Phase 2b)
- [ ] Lopmon spawns coding agent

---

**Notes:**

- Keep tool descriptions model-facing — terse, action-oriented, example values in the schema descriptions.
- Follow the `calendar.ts` file layout almost exactly; deviation only where Gmail-specific (MIME, slim projection shapes).
- No UI changes in this PRD. Dashboard → Integrations already shows Gmail as a connected scope.
